subject: Sociology
title: The Commodification of Education
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Commodification of Education.

hook.text: Universities now have marketing departments, brand strategies, and customers. Students became consumers.

buildup.text: Neoliberal reforms treat education as a market commodity. Schools compete for students who shop for value.

discovery.text: This shifts power from educators to consumers, prioritizing satisfaction over intellectual challenge.

twist.text: Grade inflation follows. When students are customers, failing them feels like bad customer service.

climax.text: Commodification changes education's purpose from cultivating citizens to producing employable graduates.

punchline.text: Education became a product to sell.

quiz.question: What does commodification of education mean?
options: Treating education as a market product
